---
title: "Quarto 4 — Cross-References and Citations"
subtitle: "Connect claims to outputs and sources"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## The required outcome — 45 minutes

> Reference the report's table and figure from prose, then add one citation from `student_work/references.bib`.

Equations are a brief demonstration.

# Cross-references {.section-divider}

## Labels carry type information

| Prefix | Output |
|---|---|
| `fig-` | Figure |
| `tbl-` | Table |
| `eq-` | Equation |
| `sec-` | Section |
| `lst-` | Listing |

## Reference from prose

```markdown
The site summary appears in @tbl-site-summary.

The generated relationship is shown in @fig-pfoa-cholesterol.
```

::: {.fragment}
Quarto supplies the number and link. Do not hard-code “Table 1” or “Figure 1.”
:::

# Equations {.section-divider}

## Inline and numbered display math

```markdown
The model uses an error term $\varepsilon_i$.

$$
Y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_i + \varepsilon_i
$$ {#eq-linear}

See @eq-linear.
```

::: {.fragment}
Use equations only when they help the audience understand the method.
:::

# Citations {.section-divider}

## Local bibliography

```yaml
bibliography: references.bib
```

```markdown
Narrative citation: @wilson2017good

Parenthetical citation: [@wilson2017good]
```

::: {.fragment}
The exact rendered punctuation and numbering depend on the citation style.
:::

## Style comes after a working citation

A local CSL file can change citation and bibliography formatting:

```yaml
bibliography: references.bib
csl: nature.csl
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Teach citation syntax with an easy-to-read author–date result first. Demonstrate the CSL change afterward.
:::

# Format portability {.section-divider}

## Test the target output

::: {.incremental}
- Cross-references, citations, and equations can render across HTML, DOCX, and PDF.
- HTML widgets are not automatically portable.
- For `DT`, use an HTML branch and a static-table fallback.
- PDF remains optional in this workshop because it adds a typesetting dependency.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Connect the cumulative report

In `student_work/my_report.qmd`:

1. Refer to `@tbl-site-summary`.
2. Refer to `@fig-pfoa-cholesterol`.
3. Cite `@wilson2017good`.
4. Render and confirm all links and the bibliography work.

Recovery: `student_work/checkpoints/04_crossrefs_citations_complete.qmd`.
